title: Kong Admin API publicly exposed without authentication
detail: GET / returns full Kong server info (Enterprise edition). GET /services returns 16 backend services. GET /routes returns 21 routes. GET /plugins returns 26 plugins (incl. multiple OIDC instances with client_secrets readable). GET /workspaces, /rbac/roles all return 200. Full read/write access. Attacker can: (1) add malicious request-transformer to inject auth headers; (2) inject Lua via pre-function plugin → RCE on Kong worker; (3) modify upstream URLs to redirect prod traffic; (4) disable rate-limiting; (5) read OIDC client_secrets (Keycloak realm VeroTX); (6) pivot to internal Keycloak at 34.71.51.91:8443.

title: Public Keycloak admin console exposed at /admin/
detail: Port 3000 redirects to https://auth.verotx.com/admin/ — Keycloak Realm Admin Console. Auth-on but exposed to internet (should be VPN-restricted per Keycloak best practices).

title: PostgreSQL exposed on public internet
detail: Same finding pattern as 152.53.82.7. Public-internet PostgreSQL listener.

title: GCP project number leaked via Kong service config
detail: ai_services route in Kong points to https://rag-copilot-614313863024.us-central1.run.app/ — the project number 614313863024 identifies the operator's GCP organization for further targeting.
